questions.stem: Write the number that is 100 less than 1,000,000.
Write the number that is 30 less than 300,000.
678 is 200 less than what number?
Write in figures: five hundred thousand and ten.
Write in figures: two million, three hundred and forty-two thousand.
What is 1,000 more than 45,321?
What is 10,000 less than 283,507?
What is 100 times 45?
What is 4,500 divided by 100?
What is 10,000 more than 395,041?
Write in figures: three million and fifty.
What is half of one million and ten?
What is the value of the digit 7 in the number 178,390?
questions.solution: In 178,390: 1 = hundred-thousands, 7 = ten-thousands. So 7 has a value of 70,000.

questions.stem: In the number 178,390.82 — which digit is in the hundredths place?
questions.solution: 178,390.82: tenths = 8, hundredths = 2.

questions.stem: In the number 217,361.05 — which digit is in the hundreds place?
questions.solution: 217,361: hundred-thousands = 2, ten-thousands = 1, thousands = 7, hundreds = 3.

questions.stem: In the number 217,361.05 — which digit is in the tenths place?
questions.solution: 217,361.05: tenths = 0, hundredths = 5.

questions.stem: What is the value of the digit 5 in the number 352,109?
questions.solution: In 352,109: 3 = hundred-thousands, 5 = ten-thousands. So 5 has a value of 50,000.

questions.stem: In the number 4.527 — which digit is in the thousandths place?
questions.solution: 4.527: tenths = 5, hundredths = 2, thousandths = 7.

questions.stem: What is the value of the digit 4 in the number 2,046,318?
questions.solution: In 2,046,318: ten-thousands digit is 4. So 4 has a value of 40,000.

questions.stem: Which is the smallest of these numbers?
questions.solution: 3,900 is the only number below 10,000. The others are all in the thirty-thousands.

questions.stem:
These decimals are ordered from smallest to largest: 10.1, 1.01, 1.1, 10.11, 11.1.
Which number comes first?
questions.solution: Ordered: 1.01, 1.1, 10.1, 10.11, 11.1.   Compare digit by digit from the left.

questions.stem: Which is the largest of these decimals?
questions.solution: 1.9 has 9 in the tenths column, which is larger than 0 or 1 in the tenths of the others.

questions.stem: Which is the smallest of these decimals?
questions.solution: 0.033 has 0 in the tenths column. All others have 3 in the tenths column, making them larger.

questions.stem: Round 263,874 to the nearest 10,000.
questions.solution: The ten-thousands digit is 6. The next digit is 3 (< 5), so round down: 260,000.

questions.stem: Round 263,874 to the nearest 1,000.
questions.solution: The thousands digit is 3. The next digit is 8 (≥ 5), so round up: 264,000.

questions.stem: Round 263,874 to the nearest 100.
questions.solution: The hundreds digit is 8. The next digit is 7 (≥ 5), so round up: 263,900.

questions.stem: Round 263,874 to the nearest 10.
questions.solution: The tens digit is 7. The next digit is 4 (< 5), so round down: 263,870.

questions.stem: Round 4,567,890 to the nearest million.
questions.solution: The millions digit is 4. The next digit is 5 (≥ 5), so round up: 5,000,000.

questions.stem: A bag of rice weighs 4,738 g. Round this to the nearest 100 g.
questions.solution: The hundreds digit is 7. The next digit is 3 (< 5), so round down: 4,700 g.

questions.stem: What number is 12 more than −7?
questions.solution: −7 + 12 = 5.   Tip: start at −7 on a number line and count up 12 steps.

questions.stem: What number is 8 less than 3?
questions.solution: 3 − 8 = −5.   Tip: start at 3 and count back 8 steps.

questions.stem:
The temperature outside is −4°C. Inside it is 15°C.
What is the difference in temperature?

The temperature at midnight was −12°C. By noon it had risen by 18°C.
What was the temperature at noon?

Write the missing number in this sequence:
−8, ___ , −2, 1, 4

Write the missing number in this sequence:
5, 0, −5, ___ , −15
questions.solution: Rule: subtract 5 each time.   5 → 0 → −5 → −10 → −15. ✓

questions.stem: What number is 5 less than −3?
questions.solution: −3 − 5 = −8.   Tip: start at −3 and count back 5 more steps.

questions.stem:
Write the missing number in this sequence:
−20, −14, −8, ___ , 4
questions.solution: Rule: add 6 each time.   −8 + 6 = −2,   −2 + 6 = 4. ✓

questions.stem: Write DCCIX in figures.
Write MMXVII in figures.
Write 1,984 in Roman numerals.
questions.solution: M = 1,000, CM = 900, LXXX = 80, IV = 4.   Answer: MCMLXXXIV.

questions.stem: Write MCM in figures.
Which number is equal to MCMXCIX?
Write XLVII in figures.
Write 2024 in Roman numerals.
questions.solution: MM = 2,000, XX = 20, IV = 4.   Answer: MMXXIV.
